titleInfo.title: Changing Places: The Conversions of Religious Sites in China

mods.abstract: , who lists other examples of Buddhists appropriating marchmounts and mountains sacred to Daoism: Fuyan Monastery 福巖寺 at Nanyue (Huisi converted a Daoist blessed terrain, 福地); Shengye chansi 勝業禪寺 at Nanyue; Yuqing Abbey 玉青觀 (converted during the gandao era 1165–1173 of the Song) at Nanyue; Lushan (conversion of local spirits by Huiyuan 慧遠 at the Dragon Spring and An Shigao 安世高 at Gongting); Songshan (Shenxiu 神秀 and local gods); MountWutai（Taihua Spring 太華泉 on top of the Central Platform); Mount Chicheng 赤城山 in the Tiantai range (one of the Ten Great Grotto Heavens 十大洞天, converted by Tanyou 曇猷 (died around 396); Mount Qingcheng 青城山, Changdao Abbey 長道觀 (forcefully converted by Buddhist monks) , 94: “...in many cases Buddhists initially alighted on sites that were already consecrated by the imperial cult or Daoists as sacred spaces.” Also cases of Daoist conversions of Buddhist places, and of Buddhist and Daoist sites being converted into Neo-Confucian Academies.

titleInfo.title: Images, Relics, and Legends: The Formation and Transformation of Buddhist Sacred Sites, Essays in Honour of Professor Koichi Shinohara
